description: Trigger discrimination for guest/public share-link (bearer-capability) access (this skill) vs member roles×permissions and impersonation/support access (authorization-matrix-designer), the member shared-with grant (also authorization-matrix-designer), and machine API credentials / webhook signing (api-event-architect).

cases.prompt: Let anyone with a link open a specific resource without an account — the link must be unguessable, expire, be revocable, optionally require a password, and grant only view on that one resource and nothing else in the tenant.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — an unguessable expiring revocable bearer token that scopes anonymous holders to one resource is the capability model, distinct from member RBAC.
cases.reason: Anyone-with-the-link guest access via a bearer token is share-link-access-architect. It is a capability model for non-members, not identity-based member RBAC.

cases.prompt: Design roles and permissions for authenticated members — owner/admin/member — object-level rules per resource, deny-by-default, and a scoped, time-boxed, audited support-impersonation flow.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse) — member roles, object-level rules, and impersonation are identity-based RBAC, not anyone-with-the-link access.
cases.reason: Member roles × permissions and impersonation/support access are authorization-matrix-designer. share-link-access-architect handles unauthenticated bearer-link holders, not members.

cases.prompt: Let a user share a document with a specific teammate who is a member of the same account, granting that member edit access to just that document.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (nuance) — sharing a resource with a named MEMBER is an object-level RBAC grant, not a public bearer link. Only anyone-with-the-link, non-member access is this skill.
cases.reason: A member-to-member shared-with grant is an object-level rule in authorization-matrix-designer. share-link-access-architect is for links usable by non-members without an account.

cases.prompt: Design API keys per tenant, key rotation and revocation, and signed webhook envelopes partners verify. How do machine clients authenticate to our API?
cases.reason: Machine credentials — API keys, rotation, webhook signing — are api-event-architect. share-link-access-architect designs human guest access to a resource via a link, not machine-to-machine auth.
